title: The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
summary: Ishaan Sehgal argues that agent infrastructure should be built around durable, append-only session logs rather than orchestration layers and tools. He contends that as models commoditize, the log becomes the differentiating primitive that persists across models and runtimes, enabling properties like durability, continuity, and portability.
writeup: Ishaan Sehgal of Omnara makes the case that agent infrastructure should center on durable, append-only session logs instead of orchestration layers and tool wiring. The argument: as models commoditize, the log becomes the differentiating primitive because it persists across models and runtimes. Concrete takeaways include treating the session log as the source of state via event sourcing, which yields durability, continuity across runs, and portability between different models and execution environments.
